programmes.show_name: The X-Files: Our Best Episodes Ever
programmes.show_description: Scully is assigned to track down a serial killer whose victims were all killed inside locked rooms.
Derbyshire's officers take part in Operation Fuego, a week-long campaign targeting road crime gangs transporting drugs, cash and stolen vehicles across the country.

Shop by phone from the comfort of your home.
Ross and Rachel avoid each other, forcing their friends to choose between them.
Celebrating the wonders of Queensland, we travel over 1,000 miles from Brisbane to Cairns onboard one of the most advanced trains in the world.

programmes.show_name: Save Money on Your Supermarket Shop
programmes.show_description: Alexis Conran investigates how supermarket clothing matches up to the high street for quality and cost. Angellica Bell uncovers how healthy offal can halve your dinner cost. (Ep2)

A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.
Paddington is impressed by a safety talk from the local fire brigade and - oh no! -  there is a real emergency. Paddington to the rescue! (VS)

programmes.show_name: **Visually Signed** Ben and Holly's Little Kingdom
programmes.show_description: King Thistle is snowed under with royal duties. He must launch a boat, judge a competition and make a speech at a festival. Can Holly help? (VS)
Grandpa Pig has built a little train called Gertrude. When he takes Peppa and George for a ride, there is room for lots of their friends.
Peppa and George are visiting their cousin Chloe and will get to meet Chloe's new baby brother, Alexander, for the first time.
Fluff loves to play and can happily enjoy endless games of Whizzy Button. For Tweedy, however, the game must end at some point, so how best to deal with Fluff's expectations? (Ep4)
Squiggle receives a mysterious invitation to go to a cake party. The only problem is that the cake is much too big, and he needs to find a friend to share it with. (Ep25)
...Big Adventures! In Brazil, Thomas meets a batucada band, who invite him to start the carnival, making great percussion sounds along to the beat.
...Normanski. Norman practices his magic act for the talent show, but his assistant Mandy is better at magic than he is.
Peppa and her family go for a day's cycle ride and Peppa instigates a downhill race.
The Browns have new neighbours, and Toq is full of tales - like there's a goblin in the garden, and her dad is an astronaut! They can't be true, can they?
A duckling begins to follow Pip around, making him feel uncomfortable. The friends realise the duck is lost and wants Pip's help to lead it home.
The Mini Patrol and the Pups race to the rescue to save a local mural before the rain comes.
The pups jump into action to build an observatory to help their friend River get a cool picture of a shooting star! (Ep31)
On a cold day, Daddy Pig suggests they build a den until Mummy Pig has finished work. After a hard day's work, the cushion den is a perfect place to watch TV together!
Auntie, Uncle Pig and cousin Chloe are coming for dinner and Peppa is allowed to join too! Peppa is very proud of staying up late - she even stayed up later than the Daddies!
Uncle Tiberius comes to visit the Rexes, and Tim must be on his best behaviour.
Grandad clears some caterpillars off his veg and safely puts them in a box for rehoming, but when Stan checks, all he finds is a strange object...
Today is the 'Twin It to Win It' pet show, where owners and their pets dress in matching outfits. But Humdinger steals Katie and Cali's tiaras in hopes of winning the trophy.
Milo, Lofty and Lark help Chief Tyrone earn a special honour at the palace after they become heroic firefighters, but they have little time to properly train.
Holly and Ben take a trip to the seaside. Holly must rescue herself and Ben when the tide comes in and they are stuck in their castle.
Night Animals: It's night time at Grandpa and Granny Pig's house, and Peppa and George have stayed up late to help hunt for slugs and snails.
Doodle's favourite grunge band comes to play at camp, but the campers need to build a windmill to give the band enough electricity to play super loud (which is how Doodle likes it).

programmes.show_name: Come & Play with Pip and Posy
programmes.show_description: Pip, Posy and their friends come up with some really imaginative handshakes to greet each other. Can the Milkshakers and Jen and Kemi come up with their own handshakes - and remember them? (Ep8)
Join Olivia for a story about a day when Georgia notices objects moving around the house. Is it something spooky!? Clever Georgia debunks the mysteries with explanations and no fear!
Start the day with a dose of hard-hitting debate, your phone calls and a little fun - all driven by Jeremy's unique style.
Two of 5's much-loved faces team up to focus on the issues that matter most to viewers - with your opinions front and centre.
National and international news.
Matt looks at the big news stories developing during the day and offers help and advice to save you money and make your lives a little easier.
Raw, unfiltered, authentic and personal, Vanessa Feltz wants you to tell her what's on your mind. Fiery discussions, a good giggle and plenty of afternoon gossip!
When Jack recognises a notorious ex-con, things take a sinister turn. Meanwhile, Piers's rivalry with Jack spirals, setting the stage for a battle of wills that threatens their futures.

programmes.show_name: New: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programmes.show_description: Lisa and Mark search for fixes to keep their motorhome on the road, Michelle scrambles to sort her Kylie costume before a gig, John readies to retire his market stall.
Join Dan Walker and the team for the news that matters to you.
Open water swimmer Mary O'Hagan found her life impacted by unprecedented algae blooms in Lough Neagh. She tells the story of how she created a campaign group called Save Our Shores.
A 44-tonne freight lorry crashes and capsizes in the middle of the M6, an HGV almost catches fire when its brakes lock on, and a school bus breaks down in a dangerous spot.
Host Jeremy Vine and team captains Sally Lindsay and Carol Vorderman are joined by former royal correspondent Jennie Bond and comedian Shappi Khorsandi to test their puzzling prowess.
Rohin Aojula helps a ewe that pushed out her uterus after giving birth, Julian Norton operates on a Dalmatian with an eye ulcer, and Peter Wright treats the oldest cow he's met.

programmes.show_name: New: Stonehenge: Secrets of the New Stone
programmes.show_description: In a TV first, Jason Watkins and historian Tracy Borman discover the answer to one of the final mysteries of Stonehenge - exactly where the six-tonne altar stone really came from.

programmes.show_name: The X-Files: Our Best Episodes Ever
programmes.show_description: In their hunt for a serial killer, Mulder and Scully try to enlist the help of an insurance salesman with psychic powers.

programmes.show_name: Call the Bailiffs: Time to Pay Up!
programmes.show_description: In London, Scott and police officers remove squatters from a council-owned building where they've been living for nearly a year. But one woman barricades herself in a room.
A gang use a broken-down ice-cream van to try and scam thousands of pounds. Secret filming reveals a fraudster's grossly exaggerated injuries.

Phoebe is distraught when her brother wants to marry a woman twice his age. Chandler tries to give up smoking by way of hypnosis.
The luxury Al Andalus train is a 5 star hotel on wheels, taking us back to the opulence of the 1930s and some of the most staggeringly beautiful cities in Spain.

programmes.show_name: Save Money on Your Supermarket Shop
programmes.show_description: Alexis Conran lifts the lid on the nation's vitamin supplement habit, while Angellica Bell investigates the truth about the carbon footprint of our favourite sandwich fillings. (Ep3)

programmes.show_name: **Visually Signed** Ben and Holly's Little Kingdom
programmes.show_description: Holly gets frustrated when Ben uses his elf skills to win all their games, then Ben becomes annoyed when Holly gets her own back! (VS)
Peppa decides that only girls can play in her treehouse, so Grandpa Pig builds the boys their own den.
Zoe Zebra delivers a very special letter for Peppa and George - an invitation to her birthday party.
Tweedy attempts to cross a 'wobbly bridge' to retrieve the Whizzy Button! With all that balancing concentration, the last thing he needs is Fluff trying to cross in the opposite way! (Ep5)
After doing the 'Follow the Leader' dance with Fluff, Squiggle goes to play a fun game with Scratch and Badger, where they each in turn get to be the leader. (Ep26)
...Big Adventures! When Gordon gets a serious case of the giggles, he hates it. He worries that if he can't stop, he'll lose the Express.
It's time for the Big Pontypandy Talent Show! But when Elvis knocks a drink over the drone control panel, the drones crash land, causing many fires.
Cuckoo Clock: Daddy Pig winds up an old cuckoo clock. When the clock strikes the hour, a wooden bird pops out, flapping its wings and singing 'Cuckoo!'.
Paddington, Jonathan, and Judy decide to create a newspaper, but they need a front-page story.
Zac wants to make friends with Mo but doesn't know how to go about it. Pip and Posy help him work out how to be friends without always being 'same-zies'.
Farmer Al and Yumi find out their garden is full of holes and their one-of-a-kind potato is missing, so now the pups must find the potato thief.
Grandpa Gravel teaches Charger all his best moves for the big dance contest. But will a runaway disco ball cost them first place? (Ep32)
Peppa and family are watching TV... until it breaks! While Grampy Rabbit fixes it, the family get a new TV which can do everything... but it doesn't beat their old TV.
A storm cancels Peppa and her family's adventure plans so they decide to take it inside. Using their imagination, they go sledding, climb a mountain and have a campfire in the attic.
When Tim and Kai have a rare disagreement, they each try to find a new best friend.
Stan keeps hearing the tune of an ice cream van but it's not there! Seals are singing, whistles are blowing and birds are barking! What's going on?
While Mayor Humdinger is getting dressed for a special day with his Hummy Mummy, the Humsquatch accidentally puts on the mayor's suit and takes his place!
When Milo, Lofty and Lark take part in the Milotown Tennis Championship, it's ball boy Lofty who shows the best teamwork off court.
Holly forgets Ben's birthday, and rushes home to make him a birthday card. Nanny has stopped all magic, so Holly has to make the card herself.
Flying on Holiday: The Pig family are going on holiday. Peppa and George pack their favourite toys and Granny and Grandpa are left to look after Goldie the Fish.
Doodle's favourite grunge band comes to play at camp, but the campers need to build a windmill to give the band enough electricity to play super loud (which is how Doodle likes it).

programmes.show_name: Come & Play with Pip and Posy
programmes.show_description: Jen and Kemi are such good friends, they always seem to know how the other is feeling. So, when Jen seems a little down, Kemi suggests a game they can play, guessing emotions. (Ep9)
Olivia reads a story in which Peppa gets to be a zookeeper for a day! She goes on an adventure at the zoo with zookeeper Mr Lion and finds out what makes the zoo such a magical place.

programmes.show_name: New: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programmes.show_description: Chef Mark juggles his growing Meals on Wheels deliveries with running his bar, Tricia and Chris battle overgrown bamboo; Paul and Andrea face issues on reopening night.

A look at the hand-harvested sea salt, derived from the pristine seawater off the island of Anglesey, that gets sprinkled on everything from Michelin-starred meals to popcorn.
A wheel ripped from a car in a high speed crash bounces across the carriageway and causes three more accidents. And a car out of fuel causes chaos in the Birmingham rush hour.
Host Jeremy Vine, with team captains Sally Lindsay and Carol Vorderman, welcome H from pop group Steps and wine expert Olly Smith for another round of puzzling challenges.

programmes.show_name: New: Alice Roberts: Our Hospital Through Time
programmes.show_description: Alice steps back to Victorian times to discover a treatment of bloodletting using leeches, and follows the present day work of a world-renowned heart surgeon. (Ep3)
We follow consultant brain surgeon Omar Pathmanaban as he tackles a rare tumour that could be fatal, and also a complex and demanding procedure requiring surgery via the nose. (Ep3/4)

programmes.show_name: The X-Files: Our Best Episodes Ever
programmes.show_description: Mulder and Scully play a deadly game with a killer who can control minds, and who targets Mulder. Can he resist?
